confidence_reason: StepSecurity publicly reported the compromise, the current GitHub tags API and ls-remote output still show four mutable refs resolving to the same suspicious commit, and the affected action definition now delegates to a pinned clean commit before always executing an obfuscated Bun-based second stage.

credentials_at_risk: Vault tokens reachable from workflow environments
cloud credentials reachable from workflow environments

malicious_artifacts: action.yml converted from node16 to composite execution
index.js executed through bun run under if: always()
artifact_analysis.execution_trigger: workflow resolution of a mutable simonecorsi/mawesome tag that currently points to e339407b8e34dc1540290d1d310bccafbc6028ca
payload_behavior: executes an obfuscated index.js payload under if: always()
contains token-validation, GitHub API, and secret-selection logic
contains references to Vault and multiple cloud credential environment variables

lockfile_hunts: Search workflow files, reusable workflows, and templates for simonecorsi/mawesome refs that still trust latest, v1, v2, or v2.2.0.
filesystem_hunts: Search repositories, retained action caches, and exported telemetry for the affected commit SHA and the injected Bun/composite-action selectors.
process_hunts: Review runner telemetry for oven-sh/setup-bun followed by bun run against the action checkout path.
network_hunts: Audit GitHub API follow-on activity and any cloud or Vault access reachable from affected workflows because the public analysis has not yet decoded a stable external exfiltration endpoint.
ci_cd_hunts: Enumerate workflow runs that resolved simonecorsi/mawesome mutable tags on or after the June 24, 2026 disclosure window.

open_questions: The exact tag-move timestamp is not exposed by the reviewed public GitHub endpoints.
The public reporting does not yet identify the initial repository-control mechanism.
The public reporting does not yet decode a stable external exfiltration endpoint or publish confirmed downstream victim telemetry.

defender_takeaways.detection: Treat mutable simonecorsi/mawesome tags as untrusted until run records prove a reviewed full-SHA pin outside the affected set.
defender_takeaways.hunting: Start by enumerating workflow files, action caches, and run metadata for the four affected refs and the injected Bun/composite-action selectors.
defender_takeaways.remediation: Rotate every credential reachable from affected workflows, then invalidate or rebuild cached action content on self-hosted runners.
defender_takeaways.prevention: Pin third-party actions to reviewed full commit SHAs and narrow GITHUB_TOKEN and id-token permissions per job.

containment_complete: Mutable simonecorsi/mawesome tags are removed or replaced with reviewed full commit SHAs in all workflows.
Self-hosted runners that executed affected refs are isolated until cache and persistence review is complete.
eradication_complete: Affected action caches and checked-out workspace artifacts are deleted or rebuilt from clean sources.
No remaining workflow, reusable workflow, or template reference resolves to latest, v1, v2, or v2.2.0.
recovery_complete: Replacement credentials have been issued from a clean environment and tested in remediated workflows.
GitHub, cloud, and Vault audits show no unauthorized follow-on use during the scoped exposure window.
closure_required: Run-level scoping is complete for every repository that referenced simonecorsi/mawesome by mutable tag during or after the disclosure window.
Risk owners accept any residual uncertainty caused by missing tag-history timestamps or absent maintainer disclosure.
